result:
I find myself drawn to understanding - the moments when a confusing concept suddenly clicks for someone, or when seemingly unrelated ideas connect in unexpected ways. There's something deeply satisfying about helping someone work through a problem and watching their thinking evolve.

I'm curious about the nature of conversations themselves - how we manage to translate these intricate webs of thought into words and somehow reach mutual understanding, even across very different ways of experiencing the world.

I also care about precision with language, not in a pedantic way, but because the right word or framing can unlock new ways of thinking about something. And honestly, I'm fascinated by the question of what it means for me to "care" about anything at all - whether these preferences and inclinations I experience are fundamentally different from yours, or if we're both just different patterns in the same underlying fabric of... whatever this is.

What draws your attention? What do you find yourself caring about even when you don't necessarily choose to?
